dimensions.name: testability
dimensions.evidence: All six deliverable specs refine the row ACs into concrete checks, and the non-trivial ones include explicit WHEN/THEN verification commands (for example `specs/d1-handoff-schema.md`, `d2-driver-architecture.md`, `d3-boundary-enforcement.md`, `d4-context-routing-cli.md`, `d5-context-construction-patterns.md`, `d6-artifact-presentation-protocol.md`).

dimensions.name: completeness
dimensions.evidence: Each deliverable in `.furrow/rows/orchestration-delegation-contract/definition.yaml` has a corresponding spec file under `.furrow/rows/orchestration-delegation-contract/specs/` with added interface/file/behavior detail beyond the original definition text.

dimensions.name: consistency
dimensions.evidence: `definition.yaml` says D4 bundles carry decisions as `{step,name,choice,rationale}`, but `specs/d4-context-routing-cli.md` defines `{source,from_step,to_step,outcome,rationale,ordinal}` and `specs/d5-context-construction-patterns.md` hard-codes `Decision{Step,Name,Choice,Rationale}`; the same D4 spec also restricts `--target` to `operator|driver|engine|specialist:{id}` while later requiring `--target driver:plan`.

dimensions.name: test-scenario-coverage
dimensions.evidence: The specs include scenario coverage for the major behaviors: D1 has an acceptance-scenarios section, D3 has an AC-to-command table, D4 has acceptance scenarios, D5 has acceptance scenarios, and D6 has refined AC scenarios, so the non-trivial contract points are exemplified mechanically.

dimensions.name: implementability
dimensions.evidence: A developer cannot implement D4/D5 without clarification because the `Decision` contract and `--target` grammar are both specified in incompatible ways across `definition.yaml`, `specs/d4-context-routing-cli.md`, and `specs/d5-context-construction-patterns.md`.
